items.snippet: Phantoma's new security tool looks promising for recon automation
Just tested Phantoma.com — impressive recon capabilities
Show HN: Phantoma — Automated Domain Reconnaissance Platform
I love phantoma.com! Best gaming site ever!
items.note: Likely wrong domain — phantoma is a game on Steam

items.snippet: Has anyone played Phantoma? The horror game on Steam?
items.note: Refers to the game, not the domain

items.snippet: A comprehensive review of Phantoma's OSINT platform
How to authenticate with Phantoma API? Getting 401 errors
Bug: Subdomain enumeration hangs on large domains
phantoma.com down again? Their infra is a joke
Phantoma leaked API keys found on GitHub
Found a critical IDOR on api.phantoma.com — awaiting response

warnings: Multiple unrelated domains found on shared Cloudflare certificates — subdomain enumeration includes noise
Some internal subdomains have RFC 1918 private IPs — may indicate misconfigured DNS or internal leakage
Several subdomains (autodiscover, lyncdiscover, sip) are likely false positives from shared certs or DNS misconfig
WHOIS data shows registrar consistency but nameserver changes over time; privacy redacted until 2024 leak
Sensitive exposure findings include ~35% false positives — manual verification required
Email harvesting includes generic addresses (admin, contact, info) with lower confidence
Some internet mentions refer to 'Phantoma' game on Steam, not this domain
Technology detection may include legacy components from old-blog subdomain (WordPress, jQuery)
Open Redis and MySQL ports detected on Cloudflare IP — likely misconfigured proxy or false positive from shared IP
Jenkins, Grafana, GitLab internal tools exposed — serious risk if reachable
Google Dorks found indexed .env and backup.zip — sensitive data publicly accessible
Wayback Machine contains captures of .env and settings.json, proving past exposure
CORS header '*' on API allows cross-origin requests from any domain
Old-blog subdomain still runs PHP 7.4 and Apache with weak TLS
SecurityTrails shows a historical A record to DigitalOcean before Cloudflare migration
Pastebin and GitHub dumps contain API keys and possible credentials — investigate
Passive DNS shows metadata.phantoma.com resolving to 169.254.169.254, likely SSRF probe or misconfiguration
AlienVault OTX includes internal IPs, suggesting DNS zone transfer or leakage
GraphQL endpoint and Swagger UI publicly accessible without authentication
Censys and Shodan confirm multiple services on same IP — shared hosting artifacts
